metadata.note: Candidate 18. Distinguishes random local outcomes from perfect same-axis anti-correlation. No Clebsch–Gordan derivation, triplet algebra, or CHSH calculation.

beats.prompt: The singlet is the joint state: spin up, spin down, minus spin down, spin up, divided by the square root of two. Neither term alone describes the pair.

beats.prompt: Measure both along z. Half the time the first is up and the second down; half the time the order reverses. Same-same outcomes never occur.
beats.label: Measure both along z. Half the time the first is up and the second down; half the time the order rev

beats.prompt: Look at either particle by itself and you see fifty-fifty randomness. The certainty lives only in the relationship between them.

beats.prompt: Why? The pair has total angular momentum zero. A state with no preferred direction looks identical under every simultaneous rotation of both spins.
